name: @cloakcode/extension
description: VS Code extension host: vscode.lm model access, transcript observer, and the localhost bridge server.

cloakcode.logLevel.markdownDescription: Minimum severity written to the **CloakCode** output channel (View → Output → *CloakCode*). Logging is **local only** — CloakCode never sends telemetry to the cloud. `trace`/`debug` add per-RPC, observer, and actuator detail (correlated by a local `traceId`); `warn`/`error` show only problems.

cloakcode.installHook.markdownDescription: Automatically install the CloakCode Copilot notifier hook (`~/.copilot/hooks/cloakcode.json`) on activation. The hook is a non-intrusive notifier (it never approves or denies tool calls); it only powers the live-pending overlay. This is a **single per-environment file shared by every VS Code window**, so the setting is machine-scoped — set it in User or Remote settings, not per-workspace (a per-workspace toggle would be meaningless: the global file already fires for every window). Turn it off to manage the hook yourself; disabling does not delete an already-installed file.

cloakcode.surfaceDebounceMs.markdownDescription: Milliseconds to wait before surfacing a pending tool call to the CloakCode client. VS Code often auto-approves or auto-answers a call within this window; debouncing lets those resolve first so they never flicker a card on the phone. Raise it if fast auto-approved calls still flash a card; set it to `0` to surface immediately.

cloakcode.tunnel.markdownDescription: How **CloakCode: Show Phone Link** gets a phone-reachable URL. `off`: use VS Code's automatic forwarding (`asExternalUri`) — phone-reachable in Codespaces/remotes, but a **desktop-loopback** URL in local dev containers. `devtunnel`: auto-host the gateway on a **private** Microsoft Dev Tunnel and parse its public URL for you — no manual tunnel or URL paste. Requires the `devtunnel` CLI installed and `devtunnel user login`. The tunnel is **private** (sign-in required to open the link), never anonymous. _Advanced:_ set the `CLOAKCODE_PUBLIC_URL` env var to force your own bring-your-own-tunnel URL.

cloakcode.port.markdownDescription: Localhost port for the CloakCode gateway (bound to `127.0.0.1` only). **Unset** (default): try **3543**, falling back to a free **ephemeral** port only if it's taken. `0`: always use an ephemeral port. A fixed value (e.g. `7801`): **lock** that port — keeps the phone/tunnel URL stable across reloads (fails if it's busy). The standalone gateway follows the same rule via the `CLOAKCODE_GATEWAY_PORT` env var, which overrides this setting.

cloakcode.gatewayUrl.markdownDescription: WebSocket URL of a standalone **CloakCode gateway** to connect to as a provider (e.g. `ws://localhost:7900`). When set and reachable, this window does **not** host its own bridge or serve the PWA — it connects OUT to the gateway and speaks only the protocol (the gateway serves the app + owns the phone link). Empty (default) = **embedded** mode: the extension serves its own PWA + bridge. If the gateway is unreachable at activation, it falls back to embedded. See docs/03 ‘Explicit gateway’. The `CLOAKCODE_GATEWAY_URL` env var overrides this setting (the dev-container F5 flow uses it to point at the host gateway).

cloakcode.gatewayToken.markdownDescription: Shared secret for the **provider↔gateway** link. When your standalone gateway runs with `CLOAKCODE_GATEWAY_TOKEN` set, this extension must present the **same** value to register as a provider. Machine-to-machine only — it is **never** shown to or used by the phone (operator auth is separate). Empty (default) = no provider auth (loopback dev). The `CLOAKCODE_GATEWAY_TOKEN` env var overrides this setting. A shared token is right-sized for a gateway you run; mTLS is a post-MVP option (docs/04).

cloakcode.mfa.markdownDescription: Operator **two-factor auth** (TOTP) for the phone → embedded-bridge connection (docs/04, F2a). `auto` (default): require a code **only when the bridge is exposed** — i.e. a tunnel is configured (`cloakcode.tunnel: devtunnel` or a `CLOAKCODE_PUBLIC_URL`); off for pure loopback dev. `required`: always require it. `off`: never (loopback + a private tunnel only). On first enable, CloakCode generates a secret (stored in the OS keychain via SecretStorage) and you finish pairing **in the app**: it shows a QR to scan into an authenticator, then you enter one code to enable MFA. Run **CloakCode: Reset Operator Access (TOTP)** if you're ever locked out. The secret is never sent to the phone or logged.

cloakcode.mfaEnrolment.markdownDescription: How the operator-TOTP **pairing secret** is delivered during first-run enrolment (docs/04, F2a). `browser` (default): the app shows the QR when you connect — fully phone-driven. `strict` (high security): the secret is **never** sent over the wire; the QR is shown only here in VS Code (**CloakCode: Pair Operator Access (TOTP)**) and the app just takes the verify code. Use `strict` if you don't want the pairing secret to traverse the tunnel even once.

scripts.package: pnpm bundle && pnpm build:web && node scripts/package-vsix.mjs
